alerts.title: Alerts
alerts.subtitle: Define threshold alerts on platform metrics. A daily sweep evaluates each rule and notifies your team via Slack and email when it trips.

rules.title: Alert rules
rules.empty: No alert rules yet. Create one above.

ceremonies.selectProject: Select a project to start a ceremony.

pm.noTasksProject: No tasks in this project yet.
pm.noEpicsAnywhere: No tasks across your projects yet.
pm.allProjectsCaption: Showing every project’s epics. Pick a project in the top bar to focus on one.
pm.depPickBoth: Pick both a predecessor and a successor.
pm.depLegend: Solid arrows = blocking dependency · dashed = epic decomposition · red = cycle (click a solid edge to remove).
pm.depAllProjectsCaption: Showing dependencies across all projects (read-only). Pick a project in the top bar to edit them.

ide.noProjectsFilter: No projects match this filter. Create a new one above.
ide.noProjectsYet: No projects yet. Pick a type above to create your first one.
ide.title: IDE
ide.subtitle: Start a new IDE project or open an existing one.
ide.parentProjectHint: Group this IDE project under a Project, or leave it ungrouped.
ide.workflowHint: LLM projects run a workflow. Assign a shared one, or build a custom one in Workflows.
ide.workflowForked: Forked into a custom workflow for this project.

tools.planSubtitle: Prioritized from your weakest area — the highest-leverage moves first.
tools.hubIntro: A free suite of diagnostics and calculators for engineering leaders — rate your delivery, estimate your AI spend, and find where to improve. Run any of them instantly; sign in to save results and track them over time.
tools.featuredDesc: The full CMMI-style assessment across six practices, with a data-driven version scored from your real delivery data.
tools.hubFootnote: Every tool runs free in your browser. Sign in to save results and run data-driven versions from your real workspace data.
tools.answerAll: Answer every question for a full result.
tools.scaleHint: How to rate — pick the number that fits each statement:
tools.saveDesc: Keep this run in your workspace to track it over time.
tools.ctaDesc: Create a free account to save this result and track your progress over time.
tools.needWorkspace: Select or create a workspace to save results.
tools.dataIntro: Scored objectively from your workspace’s real delivery data over the last 90 days — DORA, cycle time, rework, and agent outcomes.
tools.scoringProject: Scoring this project — this run will be saved to the project's rating.
tools.rollupDesc: Average diagnostic rating across your projects.

settings.title: Settings

pmo.title: Portfolio (PMO)
pmo.subtitle: Roll cost, delivery, DORA, AI-effectiveness and OKR attainment up from projects to initiatives and portfolios.
pmo.emptyRollup: Create a portfolio in the Structure tab to see the rollup.
pmo.emptyOkrs: Create a portfolio in the Structure tab to add OKRs.
pmo.cycleWarning: A dependency cycle was detected — fix it in the Structure tab.

structure.portfolioHint: Group initiatives under a portfolio to roll cost, delivery, DORA and OKR attainment up to the PMO / executive tier.
structure.noPortfolios: No portfolios yet — create one above to begin.

structure.confirmDeleteInitiative: Delete this initiative? Linked projects are unlinked.
structure.confirmDeleteObjective: Delete this objective and its key results?

okr.objectiveHint: Objectives attach to this {scope}. Add measurable key results below; progress rolls up into the dashboard.

projectLink.title: PMO initiative
projectLink.hint: Link this project to a PMO initiative so its cost & delivery roll up.

aihub.title: AI Insights
aihub.subtitle: Adoption, effectiveness and what to do next — your AI program at a glance. Open any report to drill in.

panel.aiImpactDesc: Adoption & model-usage trends, a multi-tool evaluation matrix and a composite AI productivity score.
panel.engineeringDesc: Which AI approach actually ships — outcome score, merge rate and cost by work type and model.
panel.recommendationsDesc: Ranked prescriptive actions and anomalies to act on, drawn from every insight.

delivhub.title: Delivery
delivhub.subtitle: Delivery progress, bottlenecks and engineering performance — your delivery program at a glance. Open any report to drill in.

panel.deliveryDesc: Burnup, forecast and scope creep for a deliverable across initiatives, projects and releases.
panel.bottlenecksDesc: Where work stalls — time-in-stage, the slowest stage, rework loops and the WIP stuck right now.
panel.doraDesc: The four DevOps keys — deployment frequency, lead time, change-failure rate and time to restore — vs performance tiers.
panel.spaceDesc: The five-dimension developer-productivity framework that complements DORA.
panel.benchmarkingDesc: How your delivery metrics rank against an industry cohort — percentile and rating by industry and team size.
panel.funnelDesc: Idea → validated → in build → shipped → measured: conversion, time-to-value and the idea pipeline.

eng.title: AI Effectiveness
eng.subtitle: Which AI approach actually ships — outcome score, merge rate and cost by work type and model.

dora.performanceSub: Each key benchmarked against the published DORA Elite / High / Medium / Low bands.

alloc.noGoals: No goals set for this period.

deliv.title: Delivery
deliv.subtitle: Track a deliverable to completion — burnup, forecast, and scope creep across initiatives, projects, and releases.
deliv.noDeliverables: No deliverables yet — create an initiative, project, or release.
deliv.noSeries: Not enough history to chart yet.
deliv.chartAria: Burnup chart of scope versus completed work over time
deliv.noUpdates: No updates yet — add the first one.
deliv.updatePlaceholder: Add a status update (e.g. blocked on infra, scope cut agreed)…

scenario.title: Scenario planner
scenario.subtitle: Model the completion date under a different team size, focus, or scope — and see the trade-off against the target.
scenario.attentionHint: Share of their time on this

verdict.on_track: This scenario lands on or before the target date.
verdict.at_risk: This scenario slips just past the target — within a week of grace.
verdict.late: This scenario misses the target date.
verdict.done: Nothing left to deliver under this scenario.
verdict.no_signal: Not enough pace to project — add developers, attention, or recent throughput.

lifecycle.title: Life cycle explorer
lifecycle.subtitle: Average {d} end to end across {n} recent items, by phase.
lifecycle.empty: No recent work to chart a lifecycle from yet.
lifecycle.trendAria: Average end-to-end lifecycle time per month

scopeEffort.title: Scope & effort
scopeEffort.aria: Story points completed and defined over time with the development FTE line

valueStream.title: Value stream
valueStream.subtitle: How value flows across initiatives — and where it's blocked.
valueStream.noDeps: No initiative dependencies yet — link blockers in the portfolio to map the value stream.

aiImpact.title: AI Impact
aiImpact.subtitle: Adoption trends, head-to-head tool evaluation, and your AI productivity score.
aiImpact.noRuns: No scored runs in this window.

devexhub.title: DevEx
devexhub.subtitle: Developer-experience surveys & results at a glance — the DevEx index, eNPS, sentiment and participation. Open any section to drill in.

panel.resultsDesc: The DevEx Index, per-topic scores with trend, benchmark and sentiment, the segment heatmap, participation over time and how priorities have shifted.
panel.surveysDesc: Author survey templates, launch campaigns, and view or respond to developer-experience feedback.

devex.title: DevEx Surveys & Insights
devex.subtitle: Developer-experience pulse scores, eNPS, and the AI DevEx analysis cut.
devex.noData: No survey responses in this window yet.
devex.topicsTitle: How are we doing across topics?
devex.segmentsTitle: How do we differ across segments?
devex.anonymityBody: To preserve anonymity, we only show detailed results for segment groups of {n} participants or more.
devex.noParticipation: No responses in this window yet.
devex.prioritiesAria: Topic priority rank across survey periods
devex.noPriorities: Need at least two survey periods to compare priorities.
devex.percentileQuestion: What benchmark percentile would you like your scores to be compared against?
devex.benchmarkFootnote: Benchmark data is based on DevEx scores collected from all companies that conducted a DevEx survey ({companies} in the current cohort).

benchmarking.title: Industry Benchmarking
benchmarking.subtitle: See how your delivery metrics rank against an industry cohort — percentile and rating vs your industry and team size.
benchmarking.footnote: Percentiles are interpolated against seeded industry cohort distributions (p10–p90). For metrics where lower is better (lead time, change failure rate, MTTR, cost), a smaller value ranks higher.

recs.title: Recommendations
recs.subtitle: AI-driven prescriptive actions and anomalies synthesized from your insight lenses.
recs.empty: No recommendations right now — everything is within thresholds.

space.title: SPACE Metrics
space.subtitle: The five-dimension developer-productivity framework that complements DORA.

bottleneck.title: Bottleneck Analysis
bottleneck.subtitle: Pinpoint exactly where work stalls — time-in-stage, the slowest stage, rework loops, and the WIP that is stuck right now.
bottleneck.noStages: No stage history in this window yet.
bottleneck.noAging: Nothing is stuck — all work in progress is fresh.

cloudAgentForm.name: Name
cloudAgentForm.title: Title
cloudAgentForm.tagsHelp: Keywords used for marketplace search and SEO discovery. These are tags, not the agent's Skills — assign Skills on the Capabilities tab.
cloudAgentForm.engineHelp: V2 runs the Claude Agent SDK; models route through the gateway with your tenant's Anthropic key. V3 adds the limbic affective layer on top of V2 — the agent appraises the task and executes under a fitting affective state (e.g. heightened caution on risky work).

surfaceLabel.container: Long-lived Cloudflare Container (for very long runs)

cloudAgentForm.surfaceHelp: Both run the full task in the cloud (all Cloudflare). Durable executes on-demand on a Durable Object (per step) — the default. Long-lived Cloudflare Container keeps a process running for very long, continuous tasks (falls back to Durable until Container infra is enabled).
cloudAgentForm.baseModelHelp: The tool-calling + coding model this agent runs on — pick one of your LLMs or a model from the pool. A run pins one model for the whole task; leave default to use the plan's best coding model.

modelApiSamples.intro: Call your model over HTTP. It speaks the OpenAI standard, so the official SDKs work by pointing them at the gateway — plus a dedicated endpoint for your published model.
modelApiSamples.openaiDesc: Point any OpenAI SDK at the gateway base URL and pass your model id.
modelApiSamples.workforceDesc: Call your published model directly — same OpenAI chat shape, addressed by model id.

agentPublish.validateDesc: Run a live test call against your model. Publishing unlocks once it responds.
agentPublish.validateGate: Validate your model via API before publishing.
agentPublish.knowledgeDesc: Paste proprietary documents — FAQs, policies, product docs. The agent recalls the most relevant passages and grounds its answers in them at inference.
agentPublish.knowledgePlaceholder: Paste your documentation, FAQs, or policies here…
agentPublish.knowledgeDone: Ingested — {count} chunks are now recalled at inference.

projectDiagnostics.title: Project diagnostics
projectDiagnostics.intro: Run diagnostics against this project. Each run is tracked and contributes to the project's rating, which rolls up to your workspace.
projectDiagnostics.architectureDesc: Analyze the project's repositories and score design-principle adherence. The result is tracked as a project diagnostic.
projectDiagnostics.architectureStarted: Architecture analysis started — its score will appear here when it completes.
projectDiagnostics.runOtherDesc: Run any diagnostic against this project to add it to the rating.
projectDiagnostics.architectureNeedsRepo: Map a repository to this project first.

projectDetails.brainHint: Use Brain to generate PRDs and executable task actions for this project.
projectDetails.workspaceDesc: Workspace and file system for this project. Open the IDE to edit files.

spine.empty: Nothing to plan yet. Add dates to objectives, initiatives, epics or tasks to see the timeline.
spine.anomalyTip: This item’s CAPEX/OPEX class contradicts its parent — reconcile it.
spine.descendantAnomalyTip: A child item’s class contradicts its parent.

reconcile.title: CAPEX / OPEX reconciliation
reconcile.hint: Each item inherits its class from its objective or initiative unless overridden. Verify or recategorise; the agent pre-classifies new work (adding capability → CAPEX, fixing or maintaining → OPEX).

sub.workforce: Your people and agents in one place — invite teammates, create cloud agents, and register remote agents. Publish agents to the marketplace to earn revenue.
sub.teams: Group your workforce — agents and humans — into teams, and attach a team to the projects it works on.
sub.performance: Effectiveness, engagement, and DORA scorecards for every member — human and agent — plus whole-team contribution activity.
sub.chats: Browse chat sessions across every agentHost in this workspace.
sub.approvals: Resolve approvals, questions, and feedback your agents escalate for a human — sign off on actions or answer what they need to proceed.
sub.contributors: See who — human and agent — is contributing across your workspace, and how.
sub.logs: Agent logs, execution timelines, and diagnostics across your workspace.
sub.llm: LLM usage metrics, model health, and spend across your workspace.
sub.qa: Per-project QA automation — flows, generated tests, and CI runs.

stats.effectiveTip: Effectiveness score — delivery quality (rework, reopens, cycle time)
stats.activityTip: Contribution events in the last 30 days

performance.activitySub: Whole-team activity — humans via git/PR, agents via telemetry.

consolidate.title: Consolidate people
consolidate.selectedSub: Pick who to keep — the others merge into them across every project. Reversible.
consolidate.noneMatched: None of the selected people have an activity profile to merge yet.
consolidate.needTwo: Select at least two matched profiles to merge.
consolidate.toolsSub: Suggested duplicates, manual merge, link to a workspace user, and merge history.

knowledge.subtitleSops: Document and standardize how your team works — audit-ready and trainable.
knowledge.subtitleProcesses: Map repeatable workflows and hand-offs across your team.
knowledge.subtitleDocs: Shared reference documentation your whole team can find and trust.
knowledge.trainingSubtitle: Your assigned reading and the team-wide audit trail.
knowledge.ackRequiredPrompt: You are required to read and acknowledge this document.
knowledge.ackPrompt: Confirm you have read this document.
knowledge.aiHint: Describe what to write or how to improve the document. The AI drafts audit-ready Markdown.
knowledge.aiPromptPlaceholder: e.g. Draft an onboarding SOP for new engineers covering access, tooling and first-week goals.
knowledge.deleteConfirm: Delete this document permanently? This cannot be undone.
knowledge.noReadersRequired: No readers are required for this document yet.
knowledge.noCollaborators: No one has been invited yet.
knowledge.accessViewerNote: You have been invited to view this page.
knowledge.accessReadOnlyNote: You have read-only access to this page.
knowledge.analyzeHint: Have AI review this document for inefficiencies, gaps, risks and clarity, and propose a more efficient flow.
knowledge.analyzeNoFindings: No issues found — this looks solid.
knowledge.homeSubtitle: Everything your team knows — SOPs, processes and documents in one place. See how much you've captured, where the gaps are, and what's overdue for review.
knowledge.gapsSubtitle: Recommended documents most teams maintain. Create one in a click — it opens ready to edit.
knowledge.gallerySubtitle: Pick a starting point. Your document opens immediately — name it and edit inline.
knowledge.blankDocDesc: A free-form page for reference knowledge.
knowledge.blankSopDesc: A standard operating procedure to standardize how work is done.
knowledge.blankProcessDesc: Map a repeatable workflow or hand-off.
knowledge.blankCanvasDesc: A free-form board for sticky notes, timers, images and embeds.

canvas.emptyHint: Add a block from the toolbar to start.

quality.title: Product Quality
quality.subtitle: Every error across your product — ingested, grouped, and ready to fix.
quality.empty: No errors yet. Connect a source to start ingesting.

setup.title: Error collectors
setup.subtitle: Connect where your errors come from — one collector per project.
setup.projectIntro: A project gathers its errors through one collector (one key, one snippet) — across all its repos, from the SDK, OpenTelemetry, and provider webhooks.
setup.tenantIntro: A tenant-level collector ingests a mixed stream and routes each error to a project via the mapping rules below.
setup.enableProject: Enable error gathering for this project
setup.snippetNote: Your ingest key is shown only once, at creation. Drop this snippet into every app in the project.
setup.keyOnce: Copy the ingest key now — it is shown only once.
setup.confirmDelete: Delete this collector? Ingested errors are kept.

integrations.title: Connected providers
integrations.intro: Forward errors from Sentry, PostHog or LogRocket into this collector.
integrations.sentryHint: Optional: add a Sentry API token + scope to backfill existing issues.

mapping.title: Error mapping rules
mapping.intro: Route this tenant collector’s incoming errors to a project.

surveys.title: DevEx Surveys
surveys.subtitle: Author pulse surveys, launch campaigns, and collect developer-experience feedback.

templates.title: Templates
templates.empty: No templates yet — create one above.
templates.name: Name

err.questionRequired: Add at least one question with a prompt.

dashboards.title: Custom Dashboards
dashboards.subtitle: Build saved dashboards from widgets over your existing metrics, and ask plain-English questions.
dashboards.empty: No dashboard selected. Create one to get started.

ask.heading: Ask a question
ask.placeholder: e.g. How much are we spending this month?

widget.empty: No widgets yet — add one above.

finops.title: DevFinOps
finops.subtitle: R&D tax credit estimates, SOC 1 control coverage, and audit-ready period reports — derived from your effort, cost and audit trail.

rd.configHint: Choose which investment categories (and optional action types) count as Qualified Research, and set a blended labor rate. Used for a Form 6765-style QRE estimate.

soc.seededNote: Showing the seeded default control set. Change a status or owner to start your own register.
